title: ChatGPT Review Guide — HUF Repo Structure and Priority Files
purpose: Structured entry point for ChatGPT to review the Higgins Unity Framework repo after the April 5 reorganisation. Start here. Follow the tiers. Report findings against the verification checklist at the bottom.

repo_structure.description: 316 files (312 verified + 4 S016 staged). 17 sessions tracked (S000–S016, S000-S015 pushed, S016 staged). Governance state: (CGS-2, n=3).

top_level.note: START_HERE.md is the onboarding document for new reviewers.

drafts/codawork-2026/.purpose: COIMBRA CORE — 29 files (27 + 2 S016 additions: PROOF_BURDEN_AND_MISUNDERSTANDING_REGISTER.md, BATTLE_CARD_LIVE.md). The science, the presentation, the defense.

subfolders.post-coimbra/: 11 files. Second Room material: CGS, Ramsar, scaling, confidence framework, governance declaration. Real and important but NOT for first contact at Coimbra.
subfolders.archive/: 8 files. Superseded versions (abstract v1, v2), historical documents, Off Table items (Peterson letter). Preserved for the record.

data/codawork-samples/.purpose: The evidence. Raw data and computed results. 5 files.
data/codawork-samples/.note: This is where the numbers live. Everything in the science documents should trace back here.

huf-gov/.purpose: The instrument architecture. Governance, science, evidence, tools. 51 files.

key_subfolders.governance/: Kill test, open-loop doctrine, GOV/CLS separation, safety. 7 files.
key_subfolders.evidence/case-studies/: Backblaze, energy, GDP, Planck, Ramsar, Toronto. Historical case studies.

directories.huf-cls/: Closed-loop architecture. NOT for Coimbra. 16 files.

process/.purpose: Collective reports, staging manifest, governance docs, review traces. 49 files.

directories.reference/: Historical reference material. 112 files. Machine-readable JSONs, technical notes, pillars, wiki. The fossil record — not the living organism.
directories.context-books/: 4 editions of the advanced theory book (engineering, general, physics, sciences).
directories.math-books/: 4 editions of the mathematics reference (engineering, general, physics, sciences).

review_tiers.instructions: Review in this order. Each tier builds on the previous. Do not skip to Tier 3 without reading Tier 1.

tier_1_the_science.description: The empirical evidence. If this is wrong, nothing else matters.

files.what_it_is: First empirical computation of the coherence residual on EMBER. The spearpoint finding.
check_for: Is the CR method sound or is the ad hoc normalised coupling indicator too fragile?
Are the thresholds (TV>0.05, Aitchison>0.5, CR>0.4) defensible or arbitrary?
Is the 31% STRUCTURAL finding robust or an artefact of threshold choice?
Is the Egozcue-Greenacre framing accurate and fair to both sides?
Are the six honest caveats sufficient or are we missing something critical?

files.what_it_is: Machine-readable CR results. 74 transitions, 3 countries, 8 SBP nodes each.
check_for: Do the numbers match the claims in COHERENCE_RESIDUAL_RESULTS.md?
Is the SBP design physically motivated?
Are the balance deltas and Aitchison distances internally consistent?

files.what_it_is: Cross-domain validation. 900,000+ drives, 4 manufacturers, same three diagnostics.
check_for: Is the SMART-based health classification defensible?
Does the GDoF calculation (264) hold?
Is CGS-2 legitimately reached?
Do the same pattern families genuinely appear or is it pattern matching?

files.what_it_is: Raw EMBER composition data. The input to everything.
check_for: Data integrity. Are the compositions proper (sum to 1)?

files.what_it_is: The 25-error calibration catalogue. E-01–E-17 active, E-18/E-19 future, ES-01–ES-06 scaling.
check_for: Is each error source genuinely distinct?
Are the detection tests actionable?
Are there obvious missing error sources?

files.what_it_is: S016 NEW: FFT spectral independence test addressing W-1. Three diagnostics in different frequency bands.
check_for: Is the FFT methodology sound for annual time series (N=25-26)?
Do the dominant periods (CR 2-3yr, Aitchison 4-12yr, TV 3-25yr) make physical sense?
Is the W-1 concern genuinely addressed or does the small N undermine the spectral claim?
Does this evidence support updating alignment sentence 2 to mention 'frequency bands'?

files.what_it_is: S016 NEW: Carrier impulse response analysis using Fukushima as natural impulse. Group delays, SBP phase alignment, two-phase CR model.
check_for: Is it legitimate to treat an annual energy transition as an 'impulse response'?
Are the group delay values (1-7yr) meaningful or artefacts of coarse temporal resolution?
Does the two-phase CR model (r=+0.61 at impulse, r=-0.15 at settling) survive scrutiny?
Is the SBP-as-filter-bank claim mathematical equivalence or analogy?

files.what_it_is: S016 NEW: Dependency chain analysis. Five-stage forced relay through closure constraint.
check_for: Is the relay chain (Nuclear→Other_Fossil→Gas→Coal→Solar) genuine or a reading of noise?
Does the finding that CR drops during handoffs make theoretical sense?
Is this evidence for the 'compositional disassembler' framing?

tier_2_conference_package.description: The conference-facing artifacts. Must be internally consistent and aligned with Tier 1 evidence.

files.what_it_is: 197-word abstract. Three diagnostics, CR results, Backblaze, 25 errors.
check_for: Does it say what it needs to say in 197 words?
Are the three alignment sentences present?
Would this abstract get accepted at a CoDa conference?

files.what_it_is: 16-slide talk script. 10-12 minutes. Includes CR results (Slide 10) and Backblaze (Slide 11).
check_for: Is the pacing right for 10-12 minutes?
Is the CR explanation on Slide 10 clear to a mathematician?
Does the closing carry all three alignment sentences?
Is the white flag posture correct or too submissive?

files.what_it_is: The thesis statement. What CoDa brings, what HUF brings, what only the union produces.
check_for: Does the opening paragraph carry the three alignment sentences?
Does Section 3b (CR as third diagnostic) match the empirical results?
Does the architecture diagram say 'three-diagnostic protocol' not 'dual-metric'?
Is the deployment path realistic?

files.what_it_is: One-page triage handout. Front Room / Second Room / Off Table.
check_for: Is the Front Room / Second Room sorting correct?
Should anything move between zones?
Are the three alignment sentences in the box?

files.what_it_is: Conference handout. 4 parts: CoDa primer, methods challenge, EMBER case, metric correction.
check_for: CRITICAL: This predates the CR results. Does it need a v4?
Is it inconsistent with abstract_v3.md on diagnostic count or error count?
Does Part II still say 'dual-metric' instead of 'three diagnostics'?

files.what_it_is: Validation plan. 10 ranked weaknesses, week-by-week calendar, depth vs width framework.
check_for: Is the priority ordering correct? W-1 is now addressed — does this change the sequence?
Are we missing a weakness that could kill the Coimbra conversation?
Is the depth-then-width sequencing right?

files.what_it_is: S016 NEW: 9-item register mapping every major claim to its proof burden, likely misunderstanding, and safe wording.
check_for: Are the 9 items complete? Should the wave-mechanics/filter-bank claim be item 10?
Is the 'safe wording' for each item actually safe?
Does the rewrite filter (proof burden vs misunderstanding) work in practice?

files.what_it_is: S016 NEW: 15 pressure-point questions with calibrated live answers. Replaces/extends BATTLE_CARD.md.
check_for: Are the 15 answers calibrated correctly — neither too defensive nor overclaiming?
Are there likely questions missing from the card?
Do the three anchor sentences match the three alignment sentences?
Is BATTLE_CARD.md (original) now superseded and should move to archive?

tier_3_instrument_architecture.description: How HUF-GOV works. The engineering that makes this more than a data analysis notebook.

files.what_it_is: The kill test. How HUF can be falsified.
check_for: Are the kill conditions clear and testable?

files.what_it_is: Open-loop doctrine. HUF-GOV reads, does not act.
check_for: Is the GOV/CLS separation clean?

files.what_it_is: The demonstrator. The instrument in action.
check_for: Does the title still say 'Unified Command Dashboard'? It should say 'CoDa Calibration Demonstrator'.
Does it behave as a lab instrument or a command system?

files.what_it_is: S016 NEW: 9-article formal governance charter. Governed Breakpoint Principle, Right to Interrupt, E-Stop refinement, 6 preserved rights.
check_for: Is the E-Stop refinement in Article II technically accurate?
Does the charter belong at repo root or in huf-gov/governance/?
Is it Front Room or Second Room for Coimbra?
Does it overlap with or supersede GOVERNANCE_DECLARATION.md in post-coimbra/?

files.what_it_is: Origin story. Loudspeaker physics to CoDa. S016 reframes as methodological, not biographical.
check_for: Does the BTL physics narrative hold up? Is it compelling to a mathematician? After S016, does this need rewriting to incorporate filter-bank and group-delay evidence?

files.what_it_is: Instrument description. GOV/CLS fork, PLL architecture.
check_for: Is the instrument description consistent with THE_UNION.md and the analyzer?

files.what_it_is: CR theory document. Predicted the four agreement patterns before computation.
check_for: Do the predictions match the empirical results in COHERENCE_RESIDUAL_RESULTS.md?

tier_4_supporting_evidence.description: Reference material. Consult as needed, do not review exhaustively.

files.what_it_is: The L2→TV metric correction. Proves the project catches its own errors.
Corporate identity. Correct email: PeterHiggins@RogueWaveAudio.com.
Original EMBER deceptive drift finding. Where the CoDa path started.
Maps HUF concepts to published CoDa literature.
All formulas in one place.

tier_5_collective_record.description: Process documents. Read CRPT-010 for current state; older reports for history.

files.what_it_is: S016 NEW: Current session report. Wave mechanics session: spectral independence, impulse response, dependency chain, governance charter, proof burden register, corpus revision brief. 8 collective conversation topics.
Prior session. Conference alignment, folder restructure, validation roadmap.
CR computation, Backblaze, CGS creation.
Full file history S000–S016. Every change tracked. File count 316 (312 pushed + 4 S016 staged).
S016 NEW: Comprehensive revision brief for corpus consolidation. Proposed 27→10 architecture, cooperation lexicon recommendation, S016 findings summary.

do_not_review.description: These are preserved for the record but should not consume review time.
paths: drafts/codawork-2026/archive/ — superseded versions and Off Table items
huf-cls/ — closed-loop architecture, not for Coimbra
archive/ — pre-standardised papers and old tools
reference/technical-notes/ — 20 technical notes from pre-CoDa era

three_alignment_sentences.description: Every conference-facing artifact must contain these. Verify their presence.
three_alignment_sentences.sentence_1: This is a calibration study of a compositional monitoring instrument, not a finished framework.
three_alignment_sentences.sentence_2: The instrument uses three diagnostics: total variation distance, Aitchison distance, and the coherence residual.
three_alignment_sentences.sentence_3: A 25-error calibration catalogue with detection tests and governance actions makes the instrument falsifiable.
three_alignment_sentences.flag_if_found: Any conference-facing file still saying '17-error', 'dual-metric protocol', or missing the coherence residual as third diagnostic.

verification_checklist.description: ChatGPT: report against each of these after review.

items.check: Do the numbers in COHERENCE_RESIDUAL_RESULTS.md match ember_coherence_residual.json?
Does backblaze_coherence_residual.json support the CGS-2 claim?
items.expected: GDoF = 264, CGS = 2.42, same pattern families as EMBER

items.check: Are all three alignment sentences in abstract_v3, PRESENTATION_SCRIPT, THE_UNION, and CONFERENCE_CORE_STACK?
items.expected: All present. Zero stale '17-error' or 'dual-metric' references in core.
Likely inconsistent — packet predates CR results. Flag for v4 consideration.

items.check: Does THE_THIRD_DIAGNOSTIC.md predict the four patterns that COHERENCE_RESIDUAL_RESULTS.md found?
items.expected: Yes — theory predicted STABLE, LOCAL_EVENT, COUPLED_EVENT, STRUCTURAL. All four appear in data.

items.check: Does the analyzer title still say 'Unified Command Dashboard'?
items.expected: FIXED in S016. Now says 'CoDa Calibration Demonstrator' with 'Three-Diagnostic Protocol'. Verify 5 occurrences replaced.

items.check: Is the folder structure clean? No stale files in codawork-2026/ top level?
items.expected: 27 core files. CGS, CMSI, Ramsar, etc. all in post-coimbra/. Old abstracts and Peterson in archive/.

items.check: Does TWO_MONTH_ROADMAP.md identify the right four priority weaknesses?
items.expected: W-1 independence, W-2 CR grounding, W-3 thresholds, W-4 SBP sensitivity. Is anything missing?

items.check: Is the error count consistent everywhere in core documents?
items.expected: 25 in all core files. 17+2+6 breakdown where elaborated.
312 verified (pushed) + 4 S016 staged = 316. S016 new files: HUF_GOVERNANCE_CHARTER.md, PROOF_BURDEN_AND_MISUNDERSTANDING_REGISTER.md, BATTLE_CARD_LIVE.md, CRPT-010.json.

items.check: Does the spectral independence analysis (W-1) address the diagnostic independence concern?
items.expected: Yes — FFT shows different frequency bands. But verify: is N=25-26 sufficient for spectral claims? Is mean |r|=0.231 convincingly low?

items.check: Is the governance charter consistent with the open-loop doctrine in LOOP-001?
items.expected: Charter Article IV establishes open-loop priority. Should be consistent. Verify no contradictions.
Yes — BATTLE_CARD_LIVE.md is more comprehensive (15 vs 10 pressure points, includes named principles). Original BATTLE_CARD.md should be considered for archive/.

items.check: Are the proof burden register's 9 items consistent with the battle card's 15 answers?
items.expected: Every register item should have at least one corresponding battle card Q&A. Check for gaps.

post_coimbra_for_later.description: These files are in post-coimbra/ subfolder. Review only after Tier 1 and Tier 2 are complete.
files: RAMSAR_COMPLEXITY_GAP.md — 10 deployment gaps (3 CRITICAL, 3 SERIOUS, 4 MODERATE)
SCALING_COHERENCE.md — telescoping binary balances for deep hierarchies
Q_INQUISITOR.md — Small's Q as compositional coupling quality
GOVERNANCE_DECLARATION.md — 8 known unknowns, 5 doubts, full transparency
CMSI.md — superseded by CGS, retained as development record

governance_state.w1_status: ADDRESSED — spectral independence confirmed via FFT
governance_state.w2_status: PARTIALLY ADDRESSED — two-phase CR model, simulation still needed
